page.primary_keyword: marbella boat rental for large groups
page.title: Marbella Boat Rental for Large Groups: Capacity and Tips

data.meta_description: Planning a large group outing in Marbella? Find boats for up to 12 pax, pricing from €749, and expert tips for a seamless marbella boat rental for large groups.
data.summary: A practical guide to renting a boat for large groups in Marbella, covering fleet capacity, pricing tiers, departure points, and essential planning tips.
key_facts: Largest boats (Canados 86, Ferretti 94, Maiora 26m) carry up to 12 pax.
Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours, inclusive of skipper, drinks, and VAT.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80) start at €4,719 for 4 hours, with a free jet ski.
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's main marina.
Every rental includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, snacks, and drinks.
Book at least 2 weeks ahead for peak-season large groups.
Prices for other boats are available on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is the maximum group size for a Marbella boat rental?
faq.a: Our largest vessels, such as the Canados 86, Ferretti 94, and Maiora 26m, accommodate up to 12 passengers. Smaller options like the Azimut 39 hold 11, while the Astondoa 40 takes 9. For groups over 12, consider chartering two boats or contacting us for custom solutions.

faq.q: How much does a large group boat rental cost in Marbella?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and VAT. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719, with a free jet ski included. For other vessels, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: What is included in a large group charter?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees—just show up and enjoy.

faq.q: Which marina do the boats depart from?
faq.a: All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina. This location offers easy access to the coast, with nearby landmarks like the Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and the beaches of Cabopino.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise your menu. Just note that glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

faq.q: What is the best time of year for a large group boat trip in Marbella?
faq.a: The peak season runs from May to September, with temperatures averaging 28–32°C and calm seas. For larger groups, booking at least 2 weeks in advance is recommended. Spring and autumn also offer pleasant conditions with fewer crowds.

faq.q: What destinations can we visit on a large group charter?
faq.a: Popular routes include the coast towards Estepona, Cala del Faro, and Cala Cortés, or east to Cabopino and Sotogrande. Your skipper can tailor the itinerary based on your preferences, with stops for swimming, snorkelling, or lunch at a beach club.

faq.q: Do I need a licence to rent a boat for a large group?
faq.a: No, you do not need a licence. All our charters come with a licensed skipper who handles navigation and safety. This allows you and your group to relax and enjoy the experience without any responsibilities.

data.body_html:
Planning a Marbella boat rental for large groups requires matching the right vessel to your party size, budget, and itinerary. Whether you're celebrating a birthday, hosting a corporate event, or simply gathering friends for a day on the Mediterranean, our fleet offers options from 5 to 12 passengers. This guide covers capacity, pricing, and practical tips to ensure a smooth experience from Puerto Banús.

Understanding Capacity Limits

Spanish maritime regulations set strict passenger limits based on vessel certification. Our fleet includes boats that accommodate between 5 and 12 guests. For groups of 10–12, top choices include the Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax), Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax), and Maiora 26m (26m, 12 pax). The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m) also holds 12. For slightly smaller groups, the Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) and Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) offer excellent value. Always confirm the exact count when booking, as exceeding the limit voids insurance.

Pricing Tiers for Large Groups

We offer two clear pricing structures. Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for 8 hours, with skipper, drinks, and VAT included. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 in white or grey) require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719 and include a free jet ski. For all other vessels, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp. This transparency helps large groups budget without surprises.

Choosing the Right Boat for Your Group

Consider the nature of your outing. For a lively party, a flybridge like the Azimut 58 offers multiple decks and sunbathing areas. The Mangusta 80 (24m) combines speed with space, ideal for day trips to Estepona or Cala Cortés. If your group prefers a quieter cruise, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax) provides stability and shade. For a premium experience, the Ferretti 94 (29m) delivers luxury with ample seating and a large sundeck.

Departure Point: Puerto Banús

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's premier marina. This location is convenient for groups staying on the Golden Mile or near Marbella Marina. The marina itself offers parking, restaurants, and shops for last-minute supplies. Your skipper will meet you at the designated berth, and the boat is ready with drinks, snacks, and safety briefing before departure.

What's Included in Your Charter

Every Marbella boat rental for large groups includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The drinks package covers water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava, plus light snacks. You can bring your own food or arrange catering. The skipper handles all navigation, allowing you to focus on enjoying the coastline from Cabopino to Sotogrande.

Tips for a Smooth Large Group Charter

  • Book early: Peak season (June–September) sees high demand. Reserve at least 2 weeks ahead.
  • Confirm dietary needs: Inform us of any allergies or preferences for snacks and drinks.
  • Plan the itinerary: Discuss with your skipper whether you want swimming stops, beach club lunches, or sightseeing near La Concha mountain.
  • Travel light: Space on board is ample, but avoid bulky luggage. Bring sunscreen, towels, and a change of clothes.
  • Check the weather: The Costa del Sol enjoys 300+ sunny days, but afternoon breezes can pick up. Your skipper will advise on the best route.

Popular Destinations for Large Groups

From Puerto Banús, you can head west to Estepona and the coves of Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés. Eastwards, Cabopino offers a quieter anchorage, while Sotogrande provides upscale marina facilities. For a full-day trip, consider cruising to Gibraltar (subject to permits) or simply anchoring off the Golden Mile for swimming and paddleboarding. Your skipper will tailor the route to your group's preferences.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the maximum group size for a Marbella boat rental?

Our largest vessels, such as the Canados 86, Ferretti 94, and Maiora 26m, accommodate up to 12 passengers. Smaller options like the Azimut 39 hold 11, while the Astondoa 40 takes 9. For groups over 12, consider chartering two boats or contacting us for custom solutions.

How much does a large group boat rental cost in Marbella?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and VAT. Tier B boats (Mangusta 80 variants) require a minimum 4-hour booking at €4,719, with a free jet ski included. For other vessels, pricing is available on request via WhatsApp.

What is included in a large group charter?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden fees—just show up and enjoy.

Which marina do the boats depart from?

All our boats depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella's iconic marina. This location offers easy access to the coast, with nearby landmarks like the Golden Mile, La Concha mountain, and the beaches of Cabopino.

Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks, but feel free to customise your menu. Just note that glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

What is the best time of year for a large group boat trip in Marbella?

The peak season runs from May to September, with temperatures averaging 28–32°C and calm seas. For larger groups, booking at least 2 weeks in advance is recommended. Spring and autumn also offer pleasant conditions with fewer crowds.

What destinations can we visit on a large group charter?

Popular routes include the coast towards Estepona, Cala del Faro, and Cala Cortés, or east to Cabopino and Sotogrande. Your skipper can tailor the itinerary based on your preferences, with stops for swimming, snorkelling, or lunch at a beach club.

Do I need a licence to rent a boat for a large group?

No, you do not need a licence. All our charters come with a licensed skipper who handles navigation and safety. This allows you and your group to relax and enjoy the experience without any responsibilities.

Ready to book your Marbella boat rental for large groups? Browse our full fleet and contact us via WhatsApp for availability and custom quotes. We'll help you find the perfect vessel for an unforgettable day on the water.
